method: v3_0 Phase D analytical residual (B7). Researcher decision (a) 2026-06-02: the two uncited char-47 anchors are genuine settled-wickedness exemplars and SHOULD be cited. Each is added to the host finding whose existing claim it directly evidences, faithful to the verse's verse_context analysis_note, in the finding's house style with VCG tag. The citation extractor then re-derives finding_citation from the extended text (no fabricated citation rows).

extensions.anchor: it is an internally generated and consolidated state that then overflows outward as social harm and moral contagion.
extensions.insert_text: Hos 10:13 (VCG-11) makes this internally-generated overflow explicit: wickedness is "the inward soil that produces injustice and lies" — a settled inner orientation of self-trust and reliance on strength rather than God that cultivates an inner field of moral corruption whose outward harvest is injustice and deceit.
extensions.reason: Hos 10:13 analysis_note: 'Wickedness is the inward soil that produces injustice and lies — the people's self-trust and reliance on strength rather than God cultivated an inner field of moral corruption.' Directly evidences 18414's claim that settled wickedness is an internally generated state overflowing outward as harm; faithful, in-claim exemplar.

extensions.anchor: (d) systemic imposition — Eze 7:21 (VCG-03): the wicked of the earth serve as instruments of profanation.
extensions.insert_text: (e) sacral desecration — 2Ch 24:7 (VCG-11): a woman "whose inner character is defined as wicked" channelled that settled disposition into desecrating sacred space, raiding holy things for idolatrous use — wickedness expressed outward as religious violation.
extensions.reason: 2Ch 24:7 analysis_note: 'A woman whose inner character is defined as wicked had channeled that disposition into desecrating sacred space, raiding holy things for idolatrous use — wickedness expressed as religious violation.' Directly evidences 18412's claim that the wicked extend their characteristic outward as harm/profanation (parallel to the Eze 7:21 profanation exemplar already in the list); faithful, in-claim exemplar (e).
